Title: Redox feedback regulation of ANAC089 signaling alters seed germination and stress response

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: nitric oxide; abscisic acid; glutathione/cysteine redox pools
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitric oxide promotes dormancy release and germination and modulates ABI5 turnover; ABA enforces dormancy and inhibits germination; glutathione/cysteine redox pools buffer cellular redox homeostasis and NO storage as GSNO during seed germination and stress response.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, especially imbibed seeds; NO detected mainly in endosperm and also embryos
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Gain-of-function truncated ANAC089 lacking the C-terminal transmembrane domain increases endogenous NO, lowers ABA, alters thiol/disulfide balance, and confers ABA- and cPTIO-insensitive germination plus enhanced tolerance to salt, osmotic, and freezing stress.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central redox-sensitive hub regulator integrating ABA and NO responses during seed germination and early development.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor family; N-terminal NAC DNA-binding/dimerization domain; C-terminal transmembrane domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Extranuclear/endomembrane-associated under control, ABA, GSNO, GSSG, and cold; translocates to nucleus when truncated or after cPTIO, DTT, and GSH treatment
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix Arabidopsis ATH1 microarray; NCBI GEO: GSE25159; protein binding microarray (PBM11)
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; nitric oxide status; NO scavenger cPTIO; reductants DTT and GSH; redox status; GSNO/GSSG did not trigger nuclear translocation under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Redox-homeostasis and ABA-responsive genes containing CGTnAG motif; DOG1; NCED9; GA2ox2; GA20ox3; GA3ox2; IAA30; cell-wall remodeling genes such as ATEXPA2, ATEXPA9, XTH19, BXL2, EXP3, ATPME3
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Redox homeostasis and hormone-linked control of seed germination, including NO accumulation, ABA homeostasis/signaling, and thiol-disulfide balance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gap1-1 and gap1-2 were mapped to ANAC089/At5g22290 and encode C-terminally truncated proteins lacking the transmembrane anchor, causing dominant gain-of-function phenotypes. 2. ANAC089 is highly expressed in dry seeds, induced by ABA, and its activated nuclear form promotes ABA- and cPTIO-insensitive germination and seedling establishment. 3. Transcriptomics and PBM analysis showed ANAC089 binds a CGTnAG-containing NAC-related motif and regulates redox-homeostasis and ABA-responsive genes. 4. Activated ANAC089 increases endogenous NO, decreases ABA content, alters cysteine/glutathione redox pools, lowers ROS, and improves tolerance to salt, osmotic, and freezing stress. 5. ANAC089 localization is redox-regulated: cPTIO and reductants promote cleavage/nuclear accumulation, indicating feedback between cellular redox state and ANAC089 signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant mapping and sequencing; knockout and gain-of-function mutant analysis; transgenic expression of ANAC089ΔC1-GFP and 35S:GFP-ANAC089; qRT-PCR; microarray transcriptomics; PBM DNA-binding assay; confocal localization; western blot; nuclear/cytoplasmic fractionation; NO fluorescence and Griess assay; LC-MS/MS ABA quantification; thiol/disulfide HPLC profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper's primary focus is regulatory control rather than a single nutritional end-product. ANAC089 affects multiple metabolism-linked small molecules (NO, ABA, glutathione/cysteine pools) relevant to seed physiology. ANAC089 transcript appears strongly repressed in gap1-2 microarray partly because several ATH1 probes fall within the deletion. The gain-of-function phenotype depends on loss of the membrane anchor and constitutive nuclear localization.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rises during seed maturation, highest in dry seeds, decreases after 24–48 h imbibition, induced by 48 h ABA treatment in seeds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rises during seed maturation, highest in dry seeds
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation via direct binding to CGTnAG cis-elements; autoregulatory feedback; activity controlled by redox-dependent membrane release and nuclear translocation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Autoregulatory negative feedback is supported because ANAC089 transcript is strongly reduced in gap1-2 and the authors infer an autoregulatory feedback loop; redox feedback also regulates ANAC089 cleavage/localization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activated nuclear ANAC089 is associated with induction of redox-homeostasis and germination-related genes and repression of ABA-responsive/dormancy-associated genes such as DOG1 and NCED9 in the gain-of-function background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported; altered hormone/redox homeostasis could broadly affect stress signaling and developmental programs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed imbibition; ABA; cPTIO; GSNO; DTT; GSH; GSSG; NaCl; mannitol; cold acclimation/freezing
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering activated/nuclear ANAC089 or modulating its redox-dependent release could be used to reduce seed dormancy and improve germination under abiotic stress, but caution is needed because it rewires ABA/NO/redox signaling broadly and may affect dormancy control and stress-response networks.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter pANAC089 for ANAC089ΔC1-GFP; CaMV35S constitutive promoter for GFP-ANAC089
